atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds deterministic ABI helpers for LLM fee parsing and detects encrypted LLM output by reading the base ExecutorRequest `user_public_key` field at ABI word position 4. Malformed or too-short inputs are treated as not encrypted, while valid non-empty user public keys select the encrypted response pricing path.

destinations.evidence: The destination `llm.rs` is byte-for-byte identical to the source-added file extracted from diff lines 39118-40064, ending before `registry.rs` at line 40065. Extracted source and destination each contain 941 lines with SHA-256 `f39193cf855742c15acd2c9e8c6449a180979ef0ab73ff109fed5b5e8bbb913f`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Because the entire source-added `llm.rs` body exactly matches the destination, the encrypted-output branch selection and all ABI bounds checks, offset checks, integer conversions, UTF-8 string decoding, and malformed-input error strings are preserved exactly.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: An LLM input with a non-empty ABI-encoded `user_public_key` at word 4 should return true from `has_encrypted_output`; changing the offset index or byte-length handling would route encrypted responses through the wrong decoder.
atoms.notes: This atom covers only `llm.rs` encryption detection and ABI reader behavior.

pub const LLM_HAS_ERROR_PREFIX: &str = "LLM response flagged error";

fn try_decode_encrypted(data: &[u8], payload_len: usize) -> Result<LlmPricingMetrics, String>

pub fn decode_llm_response(
    bytes: &Bytes,
    is_encrypted_output: bool,
) -> Result<LlmPricingMetrics, String>

pub fn decode_pricing_data(data: &[u8]) -> Result<(Option<String>, f64, f64), String>
pub fn decode_completion_metrics(data: &[u8]) -> Result<(Option<String>, f64, f64), String>
pub fn decode_model_metadata(data: &[u8]) -> Result<LlmModelMetadata, String>
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds LLM response metric extraction for both encrypted and plaintext executor outputs. Encrypted outputs parse `(bytes encryptedData, bytes pricingData)` with pricing data as `(string model, uint256 prompt_tokens, uint256 completion_tokens)`. Plaintext outputs support the current 5-field and older 4-field response envelopes, propagate `has_error=true` through the `LLM_HAS_ERROR_PREFIX` sentinel, decode completion usage tokens, and optionally decode model metadata using 1e18-scaled theta.

destinations.evidence: The response-decoding symbols are present in the exact-matched destination file with the same 941-line source extraction and identical SHA-256 `f39193cf855742c15acd2c9e8c6449a180979ef0ab73ff109fed5b5e8bbb913f`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves the same encrypted decode attempt, plaintext error fallback, 4-field versus 5-field envelope detection, sentinel error construction, model/token extraction, metadata parsing, theta scaling, and error handling because the full file content is identical.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A plaintext LLM response with `has_error=true` and an error message should cause `decode_llm_response` to return an error beginning with `LLM_HAS_ERROR_PREFIX`; changing that prefix or envelope index selection would break executor-error settlement.
atoms.notes: The embedded unit test `test_llm_has_error_prefix_constant_used_in_decode` is preserved by the exact file match; tests were not executed for this audit record.

pub fn compute_llm_executor_fee<DB: revm::Database>(
    db: &mut DB,
    model_registry_contract: Address,
    exec_output: Option<&Bytes>,
    is_encrypted_output: bool,
    model_cache: &mut ModelCache,
) -> Result<(U256, LlmPricingMetrics, LlmModelConfig, f64, LlmComputationTrace), String>

fn lookup_model_config<DB: revm::Database>(...) -> Result<Option<LlmModelConfig>, String>
fn resolve_llm_model_config<DB: revm::Database>(...) -> Result<LlmModelConfig, String>

pub fn llm_compute_gas(metrics: LlmPricingMetrics, config: LlmModelConfig) -> f64
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the LLM executor settlement fee path: it decodes executor output into pricing metrics, settles `has_error=true` outputs with the constant `LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI`, rejects missing or undecodable outputs, resolves model parameters only through exact on-chain ModelRegistry lookups with a per-block cache, intentionally avoids metadata fallback pricing, and converts the LLM gas formula into wei using `LLM_EXECUTOR_GAS_PRICE_WEI`.

destinations.evidence: The executor-fee and registry-resolution implementation is included in the exact-matched destination file. Source and destination line counts are both 941 and SHA-256 is `f39193cf855742c15acd2c9e8c6449a180979ef0ab73ff109fed5b5e8bbb913f`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination preserves the consensus fee behavior exactly: the same decode-error handling, constant error-fee path, trace fallback reasons, exact model-name lookup policy, cache use, registry-read error propagation, missing-model skip behavior, gas formula, rounding, and clamping are present byte-for-byte.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: An executor output that decodes to `has_error=true` should return `Ok(LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI, zero-token metrics, zero config, 0.0 gas, trace)` instead of returning an error; changing this path would silently skip error LLM settlements.
atoms.notes: The embedded unit test `test_llm_has_error_returns_constant_fee` is preserved by the exact file match; no new review queue entry is required.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +1,941 @@; input model extraction, deposit estimate, and unit coverage
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds LLM commitment-time helpers: the model name is extracted from ABI word 6 of the LLMCallRequest and must be non-empty, and executor deposit estimation reads actual model parameters from the on-chain registry, uses `max_seq_len` or a 131072 fallback as the worst-case sequence length, assumes an 80/20 prompt/completion split, applies the same LLM gas components, and floors the estimate at `HTTP_EXECUTOR_BASE_FEE_WEI`.

destinations.evidence: The model-extraction, deposit-estimation, and unit-test code is preserved in the exact-matched destination file extracted from diff lines 39118-40064. The source and destination hashes both equal `f39193cf855742c15acd2c9e8c6449a180979ef0ab73ff109fed5b5e8bbb913f`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Because the destination is an exact text match, the ABI model field index, empty-model rejection, registry-backed model parameter read, worst-case token split, fee-floor behavior, and preserved unit tests match the old divergence exactly.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: An LLM input whose ABI word 6 points to an empty string should return `Model name is empty`; changing the word index or trimming behavior would allow malformed commitment-time fee estimates.
atoms.notes: This atom completes coverage for `llm.rs`; `crates/ritual-async-fees/src/registry.rs` remains the next unaudited queue entry.
